projects.title: TODO LIST
projects.description:
# Sprint 01: To-Do List

**Duration:** 4 Days

Welcome to the first sprint!

The goal of this sprint is simple: **Build a To-Do List using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.**

This is your chance to get comfortable building something from start to finish. Don't stress about writing perfect code or making the prettiest UI. The objective is to learn, experiment, and complete your first project.

## Requirements

Your To-Do List should be able to:

* Add new tasks
* Display all tasks
* Mark tasks as completed
* Delete tasks
* Save tasks even after refreshing the page (Local Storage)

## Design

The design is completely up to you. Customize it however you like.

You can experiment with:

* Different colors
* Fonts
* Icons
* Animations
* Layouts

Make it yours.

## AI Usage

Using AI tools like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, GitHub Copilot, etc. is completely allowed.

But here's the catch: **understand what you're using.**

If someone asks you how your code works, you should be able to explain it. If you don't understand a piece of code, ask during the help session or look it up. Learning is the goal, not copy-pasting.

## Bonus Ideas

Finished early?

Try adding:

* Dark Mode
* Edit Tasks
* Search Tasks
* Task Categories
* Due Dates
* Task Counter
* Filters (All / Completed / Pending)
* Press Enter to add a task
* Smooth animations

## Help Session

A help session will be held during the sprint where you can ask questions, debug problems, or get guidance if you're stuck.

## Submission

At the end of Day 4, submit your project files or your GitHub repository.

## Wind-Up Session

We'll end the sprint by looking at different solutions, discussing interesting approaches, clearing up confusing concepts, and talking about what everyone learned. No one's code has to be perfect. The goal is that everyone leaves understanding a little more than they did four days ago.

Good luck, and have fun building!
